commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.repair_reason: - [P1] The remaining blockers are maintainer provider-policy acceptance and contributor/maintainer live proof, not a narrow automation-safe code repair.; Cleared: The diff adds provider-local TypeScript, manifest metadata, and tests without dependency, workflow, package-script, lockfile, permission, or secret-persistence changes. (sha=e652b11f7c231ac03d971fe0600d0c82def5209e)
commands.review_summary:
The PR adds an OpenCode Zen model catalog, generated manifest model metadata, runtime/live discovery, dynamic model resolution, base-URL normalization, shared credential fallback, and provider tests.

PR surface: Source +1620, Tests +355. Total +1975 across 4 files.

Reproducibility: yes. Current main and v2026.6.8 lack opencode catalog hooks and manifest modelCatalog rows while the sibling opencode-go provider has both, and the live Zen models endpoint currently returns the 48 ID-only rows this PR seeds.
commands.review_followup: - [P1] The remaining blockers are maintainer provider-policy acceptance and contributor/maintainer live proof, not a narrow automation-safe code repair.

target.title: fix(opencode): restore Zen model catalog
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: the bundled `opencode` Zen provider lacked the provider-owned catalog/resolver that its sibling `opencode-go` already had, so users had to hand-register Zen models and routes.
- Solution: restore the Zen provider catalog, dynamic resolver, catalog augmentation, route normalization, and manifest metadata at the `opencode` plugin boundary.
- What changed: the PR adds the Zen static seed, runtime discovery, family-specific routing, and tests; this update also filters live discovery to provider-owned metadata rows only.
- What did NOT change: this follow-up does not change non-OpenCode providers, `opencode-go` runtime behavior, generic LLM adapter APIs, or the shared OpenCode credential fallback policy.
- Why it matters / User impact: users can select advertised `opencode/<model>` Zen models without hand-writing every model row, while ID-only live discovery can no longer make unknown rows selectable with guessed metadata.
- Fixes #92479

## Origin / follow-up

- **Follows / completes:** This updates the existing #92495 repair branch after ClawSweeper flagged live-only Zen row synthesis in the same PR.
- **Gap this fills:** The previous PR head restored the catalog but still synthesized full metadata for live IDs missing from the static seed.
- **Consistency:** The new live-discovery behavior matches the shared `buildLiveModelProviderConfig` pattern used by `opencode-go`: filter to provider-owned metadata rows and fall back to the static seed when discovery is unavailable or yields no owned rows.

## Competition / linked PR analysis

- **Linked PR(s):** #92495 is the existing linked PR for #92479; `check-upstream-fixed` found `origin/main` does not already cover this repair.
- **Related open PR / same-contract scan:** The linked issue and ClawSweeper review identify this PR as the active candidate fix. I did not find a same-contract canonical PR on `origin/main` superseding the Zen catalog owner-boundary repair.
- **Gap in linked PR(s):** The current linked PR itself had one author-actionable code gap: live-only discovery rows were exposed with inferred route/input/pricing/context/max-token metadata.
- **Why this patch is better/canonical:** It fixes that gap at the provider-owned catalog boundary instead of adding downstream special cases.
- **Local real behavior proof advantage:** The focused provider test now exercises live discovery with a known row plus unknown row, and an unknown-only discovery response, so the metadata ownership boundary is locked in local code proof.

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** The bundled `opencode` Zen provider exposed auth/setup but did not expose a model catalog or dynamic model resolver, so `opencode/<model>` selections failed as unknown or unavailable. This patch restores the provider-owned Zen catalog, keeps a static seed visible when live discovery is unavailable or has no owned rows, filters live-only IDs until metadata is provider-owned, resolves shared OpenCode credentials for the Zen catalog, and routes Zen model families through the expected OpenClaw adapters.
- **Real environment tested:** Linux, Node 22.22.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw PR worktree. Redacted local OpenCode auth material is available from `~/.local/share/opencode/auth.json`, but Zen rejects it as an invalid API key for `x-api-key` Anthropic Messages calls.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**

```bash
source "$EVIDENCE_DIR/context.env"
cd "$TASK_WORKTREE"

NO_COLOR=1 CI=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \
  extensions/opencode/index.test.ts \
  > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-index-live-row-filter-20260619.log" 2>&1

corepack pnpm@11.2.2 --dir "$TASK_WORKTREE" exec tsx \
  "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-minimax-m27-live-proof.mts" \
  > "$EVIDENCE_DIR/opencode-minimax-m27-live-proof-live-row-filter-20260619.log" 2>&1
```

- **Evidence after fix:** The provider test exercises the current catalog surface, including the live discovery path where Zen returns `claude-opus-4-8` plus a live-only `gpt-6-experimental` ID. The result now keeps only the provider-owned `claude-opus-4-8` row, proves `gpt-6-experimental` is not selectable, and falls back to the static seed when discovery returns only unknown IDs. The full opencode provider test file passes.

```text
Test Files  1 passed (1)
     Tests  10 passed (10)
  Duration  41.47s (transform 27.91s, setup 963ms, import 39.33s, tests 743ms, environment 0ms)

[test] passed 1 Vitest shard in 109.85s
```

The current manifest row for `minimax-m2.7` resolves to `anthropic-messages` and `https://opencode.ai/zen`, and the live proof script sends the after-fix request to `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/messages` with the API key redacted. The request reaches Zen but cannot prove successful model output with the locally available token.

```text
OpenCode Zen MiniMax M2.7 after-fix live proof
timestamp=2026-06-19T03:48:06.143Z
manifest_model=minimax-m2.7
manifest_api=anthropic-messages
manifest_baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen
request_url=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/messages
request_headers=content-type: application/json; anthropic-version: 2023-06-01; x-api-key: [REDACTED]
request_body={"model":"minimax-m2.7","max_tokens":64,"messages":[{"role":"user","content":"Reply exactly with OPENCLAW_MINIMAX_ROUTE_OK."}]}
response_status=401
response_ok=false
response_error={"message":"Error from provider: The API key you provided is invalid.","param":null,"code":"UNAUTHORIZED","type":"error"}
live-proof-exit-status=1
```

Previous authenticated proof before the MiniMax route repair covered discovery and representative live completions through Claude/Anthropic Messages, GPT/OpenAI Responses, Gemini/Google Generative AI, Qwen/Anthropic Messages, and MiniMax M2.5/OpenAI-compatible paths.

```text
authenticated_discovery_models=48
authenticated_discovery_request url=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/models authorization_header=present status=200
shared_opencode_go_credential_catalog_models=48
live_request family=Claude model=claude-haiku-4-5 api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=GPT model=gpt-5-nano api=openai-responses baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Gemini model=gemini-3-flash api=google-generative-ai baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=Qwen model=qwen3.6-plus api=anthropic-messages baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
live_request family=MiniMax model=minimax-m2.5 api=openai-completions baseUrl=https://opencode.ai/zen/v1 http_status=200 stopReason=stop text="ok"
result=passed
```

- **Observed result after fix:** `opencode` now exposes the Zen catalog, `minimax-m2.7` is no longer routed through the failed OpenAI-compatible path, and live discovery no longer makes ID-only unknown Zen rows selectable with guessed metadata. The local environment cannot provide the requested successful `minimax-m2.7` model output because the available OpenCode auth token is rejected by Zen before model execution.
- **What was not tested:** A successful after-fix `minimax-m2.7` completion still needs a valid OpenCode Zen API key or the remote real-behavior-proof job's repository secret. This update does not claim `proof: sufficient` for MiniMax live behavior until that proof succeeds. Provider-owner acceptance is still needed for the full 48-row static seed and shared OpenCode credential fallback policy.

## Review findings addressed

- **RF-001 — successful redacted `minimax-m2.7` live output:** Not satisfied locally. The latest redacted request reaches the corrected Anthropic Messages endpoint, but Zen returns `401 UNAUTHORIZED` for the available local token before model execution.
- **RF-002 — stronger real behavior proof before merge:** Partially addressed. The route-level defect and live-only metadata synthesis defect are fixed with fresh local tests and redacted route proof. Successful `minimax-m2.7` live completion remains blocked on a valid Zen API key or the remote proof job's repository secret.
- **RF-003 — live discovery advertises guessed metadata:** Fixed. `buildDiscoveredOpencodeZenModels` now only returns model rows already present in the provider-owned static seed, so ID-only discovery cannot synthesize route/input/pricing/context/max-token metadata for unknown IDs.
- **RF-004 — 48-row static seed drift risk:** Disclosed as provider-owner policy risk. The static seed remains because the linked issue is a regression from catalog-backed Zen to hand registration, and it is the only offline source of rich metadata when discovery is unavailable. Maintainers may still choose a smaller seed before merge.
- **RF-005 — shared `opencode`/`opencode-go` credential fallback:** Disclosed as provider-owner policy risk. The fallback mirrors the sibling OpenCode Go catalog path and stays bounded to OpenCode catalog loading, but overlaps the open split-key policy discussion.
- **RF-006 — MiniMax proof reaches corrected endpoint but gets 401:** Still externally blocked. The latest proof shows `https://opencode.ai/zen/v1/messages` with redacted `x-api-key`, followed by Zen's invalid-key 401.
- **RF-007 — provider-owner policy and external live proof:** Current author-side code blocker is fixed for live-only discovery. Remaining decisions are provider-owner acceptance for seed/auth policy and external MiniMax success proof.
- **RF-008 — filter live-only Zen rows until metadata is owned:** Fixed in `extensions/opencode/provider-catalog.ts` and locked by `extensions/opencode/index.test.ts`.

## Regression Test Plan

- **Target test file:** `extensions/opencode/index.test.ts` plus live evidence script `opencode-minimax-m27-live-proof.mts` stored in the evidence directory outside the repo worktree.
- **Scenario locked in:** Regression tests assert the Zen catalog exposes the 48 static model rows, dynamic model resolution works for Zen IDs, shared `opencode-go` credentials populate the `opencode` catalog path, Claude/GPT/Gemini/Qwen/MiniMax families route to the expected adapter/base URL pairs, live-only discovery rows are filtered until metadata is owned, and static fallback remains visible when live discovery is unavailable or returns no owned IDs.
- **Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail:** A focused provider test locks the source-of-truth catalog/routing contract without adding broad adapter mocks, while the live proof attempt covers the external provider boundary far enough to show the corrected MiniMax endpoint and auth failure boundary.

## Merge risk

- **Risk labels considered:** `merge-risk: 🚨 compatibility`, `merge-risk: 🚨 auth-provider`, provider catalog/routing, generated manifest metadata, and large-diff catalog seed review cost.
- **Risk explanation:** The compatibility risk is that a provider-owned seed can drift from Zen if the upstream catalog changes before live discovery runs. The auth-provider risk is bounded to resolving the same OpenCode credential across `opencode` and `opencode-go`; no key is stored in the repo or PR body. The large diff is mostly generated manifest/catalog rows rather than a cross-cutting runtime refactor. The remaining proof risk is limited to successful `minimax-m2.7` execution with a valid Zen API key; the PR no longer advertises MiniMax on the previously failing OpenAI-compatible route.
- **Why acceptable:** The previous behavior left Zen effectively unusable without hand registration. The patch restores the provider contract at its owner boundary, keeps live discovery as the preferred source when credentials are present, prevents live-only ID metadata synthesis, preserves a no-discovery local seed, and fixes the concrete MiniMax routing defect surfaced by review.

## Root Cause

- **Root cause:** The `opencode` Zen provider was migrated as an auth/setup surface without owning the Zen model catalog contract that `opencode-go` already supported. Zen live discovery also returns ID-only rows, so unowned IDs cannot safely become selectable rich model rows without provider-owned metadata.
- **Why this is root-cause fix:** The patch restores the missing provider-owned catalog/resolver boundary and keeps rich metadata owned by the provider seed instead of adding downstream string special cases or guessing metadata from ID-only discovery.
- **Fix classification:** Root-cause provider catalog and routing repair for the bundled `opencode` Zen provider.
- **Maintainer-ready confidence:** High for the author-actionable source repair: the catalog regression and ClawSweeper live-only synthesis finding are fixed with fresh provider tests. Successful `minimax-m2.7` live completion remains blocked by invalid local OpenCode credentials.
- **Patch quality notes:** The fallback/default warnings are expected catalog-boundary behavior, not symptom masking: live model catalogs are advisory, and provider-owned static rows remain visible when discovery is unavailable or returns no owned metadata rows. The large diff is mostly static/generated catalog metadata needed to restore the regressed Zen catalog. Out of scope: changing non-OpenCode providers, generic adapter APIs, the sibling `opencode-go` runtime, the full 48-row seed policy, or the shared OpenCode credential fallback policy.
- **Architecture / source-of-truth check:** The provider plugin is the source-of-truth owner for bundled provider catalog metadata. This changes catalog/routing before downstream adapter selection rather than adding mirrored/persisted records or generic runtime fallbacks.
target.author: mushuiyu886

commands.reason: PR is not opted into ClawSweeper autofix or automerge
